flashcards.definition: Broad, gently sloping volcano built from fluid basaltic lava flows; relatively gentle eruptions (e.g., Mauna Loa).
Steep-sided volcano built of alternating lava and ash layers; highly explosive eruptions (e.g., Mt. St. Helens).
Resistance to flow; high-silica magma is more viscous and produces more explosive eruptions.
A fast-moving, extremely hot cloud of gas, ash, and rock fragments; the most deadly volcanic hazard.
Scale from 0-8 measuring eruption size based on volume of ejected material.
